key_highlights: Global bond yields surge to multi-decade highs, led by 30-year Treasury yield breaking above 5.5%, triggering a broad equity selloff.
Oil prices jump amid US-Iran war negotiations impasse, fueling inflation fears and pressuring risk assets.
Cerebras IPO soars 90% on debut, marking the year's largest IPO, but stock slides later as tech selloff deepens.
Fed regime change looms with Kevin Warsh appointed as new Chair, facing immediate test from 'unhinged' yields.
US jobless claims rise to 211,000 as Iran war clouds economic outlook, while retail sales show resilience driven by higher gas prices.
China's influence grows via EV tech war (BYD/Huawei), humanoid robotics race, and yuan bond issuance, as Ray Dalio warns of a 'tribute system'.

sectors.trend: Sharp selloff in chipmakers as rising yields and inflation fears drive rotation out of growth stocks. Nvidia and Cerebras face profit-taking ahead of Nvidia earnings, though AI server demand remains strong (JPMorgan hikes Dell PT). Memory stocks get cheaper despite rally.
sectors.implications: Near-term volatility likely; AI capex cycle intact but valuations vulnerable to higher discount rates. Nvidia earnings (headline 109) will be key catalyst.

sectors.trend: Oil prices surge on US-Iran war impasse, benefiting producers like ConocoPhillips and Aramco (Aramco opens $35B push). BP weighs Egyptian gas asset sale; Nigeria's Oando sees windfall. ADNOC deepens India ties.
sectors.implications: Energy sector outperforms as hedge against inflation and geopolitical risk. Oil at elevated levels supports earnings but risks demand destruction.

sectors.trend: Global bond selloff intensifies: 30-year Treasury yield hits ~20-year high, Gilts slump on political risk (Burnham challenge). G-7 to discuss bond selloff. Fed on hold path (Berro, Conference Board), but Warsh faces early test.
sectors.implications: Higher yields pressure equities and real estate; mortgage rates little changed but refinancing activity subdued. Duration risk elevated; prefer short-duration bonds.

sectors.trend: BYD and Huawei show three-wheel driving systems in China SUV tech war; Chinese EVs gain ground in South Africa. Tesla and Toyota reveal surprising auto industry truth. Ford slides as AI-driven rally abates.
sectors.implications: Chinese EV makers continue global expansion except US; competition intensifies. Traditional automakers face margin pressure from tech disruption.

sectors.trend: Grocery prices jump most in nearly four years; retail sales rise but driven by higher gas prices. Consumer sentiment fragile amid inflation. Subscription fatigue noted.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending resilient but quality mix deteriorating; discount retailers may benefit. Inflation persistence keeps Fed hawkish.

sectors.trend: Metals from copper to gold slump as inflation fears and rising yields boost dollar. Nucor analyst report available.
sectors.implications: Commodity prices under pressure from strong USD and demand concerns; gold loses safe-haven appeal to dollar.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin dips below $79,000 as inflation fears hit risk assets; prices open higher but slip. Yield farming understanding sought.
sectors.implications: Crypto remains correlated with risk-off sentiment; regulatory and macro headwinds persist.

key_themes: Global bond yield spike and inflation fears dominate markets
Geopolitical risk from US-Iran war and China influence expansion
Fed regime change and policy uncertainty under new Chair Warsh
AI and semiconductor sector bifurcation: strong fundamentals vs. valuation compression
Energy sector outperformance amid oil price surge
China's technological and financial assertiveness (EV, robotics, bond markets)
Consumer resilience tested by persistent inflation and rising rates

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration exposure; favor short-term Treasuries, floating-rate notes, and cash. Consider TIPS for inflation protection.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: US-Iran War Escalation and Oil Price Shock
risk_assessment.mitigation: Overweight energy sector (XLE, OIH) and commodities; hedge with oil futures or options. Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and airlines.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Fed Policy Mistake Under New Chair Warsh
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify across geographies; increase allocation to non-US equities (e.g., emerging markets value) and real assets. Monitor Fed communication closely.
Reduce exposure to sectors reliant on China supply chains; consider onshoring beneficiaries. Maintain strategic allocation to defense and cybersecurity.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Inflation Persistence and Consumer Spending Slowdown
risk_assessment.mitigation: Focus on companies with pricing power and low input cost sensitivity. Avoid highly leveraged consumer names. Favor value and dividend growers.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Tech Valuation Correction Amid Rising Yields
risk_assessment.mitigation: Trim high-multiple growth stocks; rotate to quality tech with strong cash flows and reasonable valuations. Use put spreads for hedging.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy energy sector ETFs (XLE, OIH) and select oil producers (ConocoPhillips, Aramco via ADRs)
opportunities.rationale: Oil prices elevated due to geopolitical risk and supply constraints; energy sector offers inflation hedge and strong cash flows.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate high-quality AI infrastructure plays on pullback (Nvidia, Dell, Kioxia)
opportunities.rationale: AI capex cycle remains robust; JPMorgan hikes Dell PT, memory demand strong. Near-term volatility creates entry points ahead of Nvidia earnings.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider short-duration Treasury ETFs (SHV, BIL) and floating-rate notes
opportunities.rationale: Yields at multi-decade highs provide attractive income with minimal duration risk; Fed on hold path supports carry.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy gold on dips as hedge against geopolitical and inflation risks
opportunities.rationale: Gold slumped recently but remains a strategic hedge; central bank buying and geopolitical uncertainty support long-term demand.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-growth tech and small-cap stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising yields compress valuations; small caps and unprofitable tech are most vulnerable. Rotate to large-cap quality.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation to 10-15% of portfolio
defensive_moves.rationale: Cash provides optionality to deploy during further selloffs; yields on cash (money market) are attractive at ~5%.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge equity portfolios with put spreads on S&P 500 (SPY)
defensive_moves.rationale: Elevated volatility and downside risk from bond yields and geopolitical events warrant tail-risk protection.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Diversify into non-US equities, particularly emerging markets value
defensive_moves.rationale: EM stocks fell sharply but offer attractive valuations; Colombian GDP beat and India energy deals show selective opportunities.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets remain under pressure from rising bond yields, inflation fears, and geopolitical uncertainty. The S&P 500 could test key support levels (around 5,000) if yields continue to climb. Nvidia earnings (May 20) and G-7 discussions on bond selloff are critical near-term catalysts. Volatility likely elevated; defensive positioning warranted.
market_outlook.long_term: Inflation persistence and Fed regime change under Warsh suggest a higher-for-longer rate environment. However, AI-driven productivity gains and energy sector strength provide structural growth. Long-term investors should focus on quality, pricing power, and energy exposure. China's technological rise and geopolitical realignment will reshape global supply chains and investment flows.
key_catalysts: G-7 meeting to discuss bond selloff (headline 51)
Kevin Warsh's first Fed policy signals (headlines 34, 43, 76)
May CPI and PCE inflation data releases
Cerebras and other AI IPO aftermarket performance (headlines 15, 100, 106)
watch_list: Retail sales and consumer confidence data
China EV market share in Europe and emerging markets
